category_title: Preschoolers engaging in loud, chaotic, 'trashing' play
scenario_title: Escalation into chaotic 'trashing' and animalistic play inside constructed tent
minimal_context: The preschoolers are gathering materials and initiating the 'Sleeping Family Game' inside a makeshift tent during morning free play.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: It looks like you are gathering materials for your tent in the dramatic play area.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: Let's make a tent! (Fills a pushcart with the entire contents of the dramatic play kitchen and dumps it all into the tent, on top of some members of the Sleeping Family.)

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: You brought all the dishes and plastic food inside.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: Now we have food! (Crawls back into the tent and noisily pretends to eat.)

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: You are our kitty and puppy! (Engages in ravenous and animalistic eating with loud gnashings and growlings, throws the plastic food hither and yon, removes items of clothing, and begins growling, meowing and barking whilst rolling about on the floor.)

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: It looks like you are gathering materials for your tent in the dramatic play area.

STUDENT: Let's make a tent! (Fills a pushcart with the entire contents of the dramatic play kitchen and dumps it all into the tent, on top of some members of the Sleeping Family.)

TUTOR: You brought all the dishes and plastic food inside.

STUDENT: Now we have food! (Crawls back into the tent and noisily pretends to eat.)

TUTOR: How is your food?

STUDENT: You are our kitty and puppy! (Engages in ravenous and animalistic eating with loud gnashings and growlings, throws the plastic food hither and yon, removes items of clothing, and begins growling, meowing and barking whilst rolling about on the floor.)

task.content: Initiating the 'Sleeping Family Game' during morning free play, assigning fluid familial roles, and gathering materials to furnish a makeshift tent.

context.environment: Center of the classroom in the dramatic play area, using an upended wooden storefront structure.
context.materials: Sheets, blankets, pillows, seat cushions, long scarves, dress-up clothes, pushcart, plastic food, dishes, play money, dress-up shoes, beaded necklaces, hats, and plastic baby dolls.
context.social_setting: Group of preschoolers (Isaac, Martina, Bonnie, Tucker, Josefa) playing together during morning free play.
context.time_context: Just before 9 am during the two-hour morning free play period.

challenging_behavior_trigger.condition: When the children crawl back into the tent to noisily pretend to eat the gathered food.
challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: Engaged in ravenous and animalistic eating with loud gnashings and growlings. Threw plastic food hither and yon, gathered large amounts of small plastic toys in their arms and threw them in the air inside the tent. Removed items of their clothing and their shoes, squealed and howled with delight, and began growling, meowing and barking whilst rolling about on the floor.
challenging_behavior_trigger.internal_state: Joyous, revelling in their transitory resistant experience of relationships, sensation and unruly love.
related_quotes: You are our kitty and puppy

behavior_patterns.condition: When gathering materials to furnish the tent.
behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: Filled a pushcart with the entire contents of the dramatic play kitchen and home area, and dumped it all into the tent, often on top of some members of the Sleeping Family.
